target.name: Porotech
target.founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Porotech is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector at the same stage, anchored by companies including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M), alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $20M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a $40M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four largest private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring (P/S median of 20.6x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data for Porotech.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $34M to $1.7B, with a median of $770M. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around market positioning relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method yields the low end, while peer-median anchoring dominates the median and high-end estimates. The key uncertainty is Porotech's relative technology maturity, product-market traction, and capital intensity relative to the four primary comparables used in the peer-median approach.
